title: Quiz: History of NIST PQC Initiative

questions.question: In what year did Peter Shor publish his algorithm that threatens current public-key cryptography?
questions.explanation: Peter Shor published his quantum factoring algorithm in 1994, demonstrating that a sufficiently powerful quantum computer could break RSA, Diffie-Hellman, and elliptic curve cryptography.

questions.question: When did NIST issue its call for post-quantum cryptography proposals?
questions.explanation: NIST issued its formal call for post-quantum cryptographic algorithm proposals on December 20, 2016.

questions.question: How many complete submissions did NIST receive by the November 2017 deadline?
questions.explanation: NIST received 82 complete submissions by the November 30, 2017 deadline, including 59 encryption/KEM schemes and 23 signature schemes.

questions.question: Which post-quantum cryptographic approach has the longest history, dating back to 1978?
questions.explanation: The McEliece cryptosystem, a code-based scheme, was published in 1978, making it the oldest post-quantum cryptographic approach still considered secure.

questions.question: What happened to SIKE in August 2022?

options.text: It was selected as a NIST standard
It was completely broken by a classical attack

questions.explanation: In August 2022, SIKE was completely broken by a polynomial-time classical attack discovered by Castryck and Decru. The attack runs on a standard laptop, demonstrating a fundamental flaw in the scheme.

questions.question: Which three FIPS standards were published on August 13, 2024?
questions.explanation: FIPS 203 (ML-KEM), FIPS 204 (ML-DSA), and FIPS 205 (SLH-DSA) were published on August 13, 2024, as the first post-quantum cryptographic standards.

questions.question: The NIST PQC standardization process took approximately 8 years from the initial call to the final standards.
questions.explanation: The process began with NIST's call for proposals in December 2016 and concluded with the publication of final standards in August 2024, spanning approximately 8 years.
